title: Logical properties and writing modes

publication.changeSummary: Technology-specific CSS: Responsive and Accessible Styling draft for Logical properties and writing modes; independent standards, browser, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: CSS Snapshot 2024; W3C Working Group Note 2024; provisions applicable to Logical properties and writing modes
sources.claim: Logical properties express block and inline dimensions or edges so layout adapts to writing mode and text direction.

sources.locator: MDN CSS Guide; accessed 2026-07; provisions applicable to Logical properties and writing modes
sources.claim: Logical properties express block and inline dimensions or edges so layout adapts to writing mode and text direction.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Logical properties express block and inline dimensions or edges so layout adapts to writing mode and text direction.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A card uses margin-inline-start instead of margin-left so spacing mirrors in right-to-left text. Your goal is to refactor directional properties and verify a component in left-to-right and right-to-left contexts. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Box model, sizing and overflow; html semantics landmarks; Normal flow and display; Lengths, percentages and viewport units. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Technology rule
sections.body: Logical properties express block and inline dimensions or edges so layout adapts to writing mode and text direction. Apply this rule to the actual markup, stylesheet, JavaScript, or component state rather than memorizing its name.

sections.heading: Boundary and browser reality
sections.body: Some visuals remain physically oriented, mixed-direction content needs testing, and source order still determines navigation. This draft therefore rejects: RTL support is complete when text-align is switched to right.

includes: Use block/inline-relative sizing and spacing for direction- and language-resilient layouts.
Refactor directional properties and verify a component in left-to-right and right-to-left contexts.
excludes: Some visuals remain physically oriented, mixed-direction content needs testing, and source order still determines navigation.

outcomes: Refactor directional properties and verify a component in left-to-right and right-to-left contexts.

examples.title: Logical properties and writing modes: concrete case
examples.situation: A card uses margin-inline-start instead of margin-left so spacing mirrors in right-to-left text.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the exact artifact and starting state: A card uses margin-inline-start instead of margin-left so spacing mirrors in right-to-left text.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: Use logical properties and test direction, writing mode, content order, icons, and interactions rather than flipping alignment alone.
examples.result: The result follows because Use logical properties and test direction, writing mode, content order, icons, and interactions rather than flipping alignment alone.

Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Reject the misconception: RTL support is complete when text-align is switched to right.
examples.result: The boundary requires this correction: Use logical properties and test direction, writing mode, content order, icons, and interactions rather than flipping alignment alone.

misconceptions.mistakenBelief: RTL support is complete when text-align is switched to right.
misconceptions.correction: Use logical properties and test direction, writing mode, content order, icons, and interactions rather than flipping alignment alone.
misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Use a concrete CSS: Responsive and Accessible Styling artifact to explain why this belief fails: RTL support is complete when text-align is switched to right.

analogy.example: A layered stage design can model Logical properties and writing modes, where rules size, place, and paint existing content.
usefulFor: Building a first mental model for Logical properties and writing modes before using exact platform terms
limitations: Physical layers do not reproduce cascade algorithms, intrinsic sizing, writing modes, or user preferences.
